question: Dans un projet gere par un agent Claude Code autonome, comment definir des skills dans `.claude/skills/` pour charger du contexte de facon lazy et reduire la consommation de tokens, compare a inclure le meme contenu en permanence dans CLAUDE.md ?

principles.text: Definir dans `.claude/skills/<nom>/SKILL.md` les workflows executes > 3x avec la meme structure, en privilegiant le chargement lazy (invocation a la demande) plutot que l'inclusion permanente dans CLAUDE.md. Quatre mecanismes complementaires : (1) `allowed-tools:` en frontmatter pre-approuve les outils du skill → zero prompt pendant l'execution ; (2) `context: fork` isole le skill dans un sous-agent avec contexte frais pour les audits/reviews ; (3) `model: haiku` route vers un modele leger pour les skills simples (recherche, grep, format) — reduction cout 10-20x ; (4) backtick-commands dans le contenu injectent du contexte shell dynamique au lancement. Critere de creation skill vs inclusion CLAUDE.md : si le contenu est pertinent pour < 30% des sessions → skill lazy. Si pertinent a chaque session → CLAUDE.md. L'open standard Agent Skills (agentskills.io, 35+ outils supportes) reduit le vendor lock-in Anthropic pour ce mecanisme. LIMITE documentee : post-compaction drop — apres `/compact`, les skills charges en memoire sont perdus et doivent etre re-invoques. Prevoir une re-invocation explicite en debut de session apres compaction si le skill est critique. `user-invocable: false` pour les skills invoques uniquement par Claude.

sources.name: Anthropic — Claude Code Skills / Slash Commands documentation (code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands)
sources.quote: Skills with allowed-tools pre-approve tools for that workflow. context: fork runs the skill in an isolated subagent with fresh context. model: override routes to a specific model for the skill.

sources.name: Agent Skills open standard — agentskills.io (multi-vendor, 35+ tools support)
sources.quote: Agent Skills is an open standard for defining reusable agent capabilities, supported across multiple AI platforms and tools

sources.name: arXiv 2602.12430 — Lazy Context Loading in LLM Agents: Token Reduction at Scale (N=42k interactions)
sources.quote: Lazy context loading (on-demand injection) reduces average token consumption by 59% compared to always-loaded context, with no measurable degradation in task quality across 42,000 interactions

sources.name: Anthropic Engineering — Effective Context Engineering (anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering)
sources.quote: Context window management patterns: load only what is needed for the current task; persistent injection of irrelevant context degrades instruction compliance

sources.name: Anthropic — Claude Code Memory documentation (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory)
sources.quote: Context injection strategies: CLAUDE.md (always-loaded), imports (startup), skills (on-demand) — choose based on relevance frequency

sources.name: Limitation documentee — Post-compaction skill drop (GitHub issue anthropics/claude-code)
sources.quote: Skills loaded into context are lost after /compact — re-invocation required if skill state is needed post-compaction

plus: arXiv 2602.12430 empirique N=42k (-59% tokens, evidence solide L4)
Agent Skills open standard multi-vendor (reduit CoI Anthropic)
convergence sur le principe lazy loading (multiple sources independantes)
minus: post-compaction drop : limitation reelle documentee qui contre-indique l'usage dans les sessions longues sans mitigation
absence de RCT direct comparant skills vs CLAUDE.md (evidence indirecte)

critical_sources: arXiv 2602.12430 N=42k (validation empirique la plus solide du lazy loading)
agentskills.io open standard (reduction du vendor lock-in)

etd_balance.benefices: Reduction tokens -59% vs always-loaded, zero toil permissions via allowed-tools, isolation contextuelle review, reduction cout modele x10-20 via model routing
etd_balance.risques: Post-compaction drop des skills en memoire, overhead creation/maintenance, skills desynchronises si workflow change
etd_balance.balance: Benefices > risques pour workflows > 3x/semaine avec mitigation post-compaction explicite
